title: Agrodome Farm Show Review
meta_desc: Discover the Agrodome Farm Show in Rotorua, a must-visit for families with kids aged 2+. Learn about its value for money, insider tips, and how to save money on tickets.
intro: The Agrodome Farm Show is a classic Rotorua attraction that's been entertaining families for over 50 years. With its interactive shows and hands-on animal encounters, it's an ideal activity for kids aged 2 and above. But what's it really like to visit with kids, and is it worth the price?

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.body:
The Agrodome Farm Show is a 1.5-hour show that's fully undercover, making it perfect for rainy days. Kids will love feeding lambs, watching sheep being shorn, and seeing sheepdogs in action. The show is very interactive, with plenty of opportunities for kids to get involved and ask questions.

The farm itself is a working sheep and cattle farm, so kids can see real farm animals up close. The show is hosted by experienced farmers who are passionate about sharing their knowledge with visitors.
list: Feed lambs and pet other farm animals
Watch sheep being shorn and learn about the process
See sheepdogs in action, herding sheep with ease

sections.heading: Value for Money and Age Suitability
sections.body:
Tickets to the Agrodome Farm Show cost $44 per adult and $25 per child, which is reasonable considering the length and quality of the show. The show is suitable for kids aged 2 and above, and we'd rate it 10/10 for family suitability.

However, some parents may find the show a bit pricey, especially for larger families. But with its unique blend of education and entertainment, we think it's worth the cost.

sections.heading: Insider Tips and Best Time to Visit
sections.body:
To make the most of your visit, we recommend arriving early to explore the farm and its animals before the show starts. You can also book a farm tour or animal encounter for an extra fee.

The best time to visit the Agrodome Farm Show is during the week, when it's less crowded. If you can, avoid visiting during school holidays or peak season, when prices may be higher and the show may be more crowded.
list: Arrive early to explore the farm and its animals
Book a farm tour or animal encounter for an extra fee
Visit during the week for smaller crowds

sections.heading: How to Book and Save Money
sections.body:
You can book tickets to the Agrodome Farm Show online or at the door. To save money, consider booking a family pass or looking for special deals and discounts on the Agrodome website.

Some online booking platforms may also offer discounts or package deals that include other Rotorua attractions. Be sure to compare prices and look for any available discounts before booking your tickets.
list: Book online or at the door
Consider a family pass for discounts
Look for special deals and discounts on the Agrodome website
